tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut. The MTA is governed by a 23-member Board of Directors, organized in eight committees.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network
The MTA is governed by a 23-member Board of Directors, organized in eight committees.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: The Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign launched in late June of this year.

payload.description: Launched in late June 2022. Simplified routes (Bx11, Bx24, Bx36, and Bx40/Bx42) have seen reduced travel times. Daily and weekly average speeds increased 4% on the 13 modified routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign launched in late June of this year.

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Bus Redesign Draft Plan
tool_args.raw_text: Following on our success in the Bronx, the Brooklyn Bus Redesign Draft Plan will be released by the end of 2022.

payload.description: Draft Plan to be released by end of 2022, followed by public outreach events starting in early 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the Brooklyn Bus Redesign Draft Plan will be released by the end of 2022.

tool_args.label: Open Stroller Pilot Program
tool_args.raw_text: September marked the successful launch of the MTA's Open Stroller Pilot Program

payload.description: Launched September 2022. Customers with strollers can board buses marked with a stroller decal and safely park their open stroller in a dedicated on-board space. 140+ buses with dedicated stroller spaces across 7 routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: September marked the successful launch of the MTA's Open Stroller Pilot Program
140+ Buses with Dedicated Stroller Spaces … 7 Routes

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) Camera Expansion
tool_args.raw_text: MTA and NYC DOT have agreed to expand the ABLE program to cover up to 85% of existing bus lanes with the installation of additional cameras on buses, which will total 1,000 by the end of 2023.

payload.description: Expansion of ABLE cameras: 300 new cameras installed by end of 2022 on 9 routes in outer boroughs; goal of 1,000 cameras by end of 2023 covering 85% of bus lanes. First cameras implemented on Q44-SBS and S79-SBS routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA and NYC DOT have agreed to expand the ABLE program to cover up to 85% of existing bus lanes
300 New ABLE Cameras Installed by End of 2022 … 85% Bus Lanes Covered by ABLE by End of 2023
The expansion of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement cameras will cover 300 additional buses on nine routes

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.raw_text: September marked the successful launch of the MTA's Open Stroller Pilot Program, which will allow customers with strollers to board buses marked with a stroller decal outside the bus and safely park their open stroller with their child inside in a dedicated on-board space.
